QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_34

question.text: In an IPO where the majority of issue proceeds (excluding OFS component) are used for capital expenditure, what is the lock-in period for promoter holdings IN EXCESS of the minimum contribution?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** When the majority of issue proceeds (excluding OFS) are earmarked for capital expenditure, the lock-in structure is stricter: minimum contribution is locked for 3 years, and the EXCESS over minimum is locked for 1 year (instead of the standard 6 months).
**The Trap:** 6 months is the standard lock-in for excess holdings when proceeds are NOT primarily for capex. In the capex-heavy scenario, this extends to 1 year. Option C (3 years) is the lock-in for minimum contribution, not the excess.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX frequently tests the matrix of lock-in periods — candidates must know both the minimum and excess lock-in under both capex and non-capex scenarios.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Create a 2x2 matrix: (minimum vs excess) x (capex majority vs non-capex). This gives you 18m/6m for non-capex and 3y/1y for capex-heavy issues. This mental model saves time in the exam.
